segments.text: Lesson 17.

translations.zh-Hans: 第17课。

segments.text: A Man-made Disease.

translations.zh-Hans: 人为的疾病。

segments.text: First listen and then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What factor helped to spread the disease of myxomatosis?

translations.zh-Hans: 什么因素帮助传播了黏液瘤病？

segments.text: In the early days of the settlement of Australia, enterprising settlers unwisely introduced the European rabbit.

translations.zh-Hans: 澳大利亚殖民初期，富有进取心的移民不明智地引进了欧洲野兔。

segments.text: This rabbit had no natural enemies in the Antipodus, so that it multiplied with that promiscuous abandoned characteristic of rabbits.

translations.zh-Hans: 这种兔子在南半球没有天敌，于是以兔子特有的毫无节制的方式大量繁殖。

segments.text: It overran a whole continent.

translations.zh-Hans: 它们遍布了整个大陆。

segments.text: It caused devastation by burrowing and by devouring the herbage which might have maintained millions of sheep and cattle.

translations.zh-Hans: 它们挖洞，并吞食本可供数百万牛羊食用的草木，造成了严重破坏。

segments.text: discovered that this particular variety of rabbit, and apparently no other animal, was susceptible to a fatal virus disease, myxomatosis.

translations.zh-Hans: 后来人们发现，只有这种兔子、显然没有其他动物，会感染一种致命的病毒性疾病——黏液瘤病。

segments.text: By infecting animals and letting them loose in the burrows, local epidemics of this disease could be created.

translations.zh-Hans: 把动物感染后放进兔穴，就能在当地制造这种疾病的流行。

segments.text: Later it was found that there was a type of mosquito which acted as the carrier of this disease and passed it on to the rabbits.

translations.zh-Hans: 后来又发现，有一种蚊子充当这种疾病的传播媒介，把病传给兔子。

segments.text: So while the rest of the world was trying to get rid of Australia was encouraging this one.

translations.zh-Hans: 因此，当世界其他地方都在努力消灭蚊子时，澳大利亚却在鼓励这一种蚊子繁殖。

segments.text: It effectively spread the disease all over the continent and drastically reduced the rabbit population.

translations.zh-Hans: 它有效地把疾病传遍整个大陆，使兔子数量锐减。

segments.text: It later became apparent that rabbits were developing a degree of resistance to this disease, so that the rabbit population was unlikely to be completely exterminated.

translations.zh-Hans: 后来逐渐明显的是，兔子正在形成一定程度的抗病能力，因此兔群不大可能被彻底消灭。

segments.text: There were hopes, however, that the problem of the rabbit would become manageable.

translations.zh-Hans: 不过，人们仍希望兔患能变得可以控制。

segments.text: Ironically, Europe, which had bequeathed the rabbit as a pest to Australia, acquired this man-made disease as a pestilence.

translations.zh-Hans: 具有讽刺意味的是，欧洲把兔子这种祸害送给澳大利亚后，自己却又把这种人为疾病当作瘟疫接了回来。

segments.text: A French physician decided to get rid of the wild rabbits on his own estate and introduced myxomatosis.

translations.zh-Hans: 一位法国医生决定消灭自家庄园里的野兔，于是引入了黏液瘤病。

segments.text: It did not, however, remain within the confines of his estate.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而，它并没有局限在他的庄园范围内。

segments.text: It spread through France, where wild rabbits are not generally regarded as a pest, but as a sport and a useful food supply, and it spread to Britain, where wild rabbits are regarded as a pest, but where domesticated

translations.zh-Hans: 疾病传遍法国；在那里，野兔通常不被视为祸害，而被看作狩猎对象和有用的食物来源。疾病也传到英国；那里虽把野兔视为祸害，但家养

segments.text: rabbits, equally susceptible to the disease, are the basis of a profitable fur industry.

translations.zh-Hans: 兔同样容易染病，而它们又是有利可图的毛皮业的基础。

segments.text: The question became one of whether man could control the disease he had invented.

translations.zh-Hans: 问题变成了：人类能否控制自己创造的疾病。
